title: Critoniadelphus microdon
taxonomy: Critoniadelphus microdon is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the tribe Eupatorieae. The plant is a member of the genus Critoniadelphus, which is part of the larger clade of flowering plants known as angiosperms. Critoniadelphus microdon is native to South America and is commonly found in the regions of Argentina and Uruguay. Its classification reflects its close relationship with other plants in the Eupatorieae tribe.

It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in wound healing and reducing symptoms of infections.
